assertions.text: Removes 'Think through' prompts, scaffold elements, status markers, and Raw Material sections
Title is the sharpest or most memorable line from the lesson, not a generic description
Output is clean prose suitable for Substack — no bullet-only sections, no scaffold artifacts
Maintains the author's honest, direct tone — including uncertainty and 'what I'm still figuring out'
Includes a setup paragraph framing the problem for readers who don't know the context
Includes a one-line bio at the end
Does not invent examples, anecdotes, or claims not present in the source lesson
